The calculations are as follows: EV = EV of activity 1 + EV of activity 2 + EV of activity 3 = (1 x $15,000) + (1 x $10,000) + (0.50 x $250,000) = $150,000 AC = AC of activity 1 + AC of activity 2 + AC of activity 3 = $14,000 + $11,000 + $175,000 = $200,000 TCPI = (BAC - EV) / (EAC - AC) = ($500,000 - $150,000) / ($600,000 - $200,000) = 0.88 The TCPI of 0.88 means that in order to complete the project with the new budget of EAC = $600,000 instead

questions.prompt: You are running a project to manufacture high-precision engine sub-assemblies. Your project team is about to start internal quality testing which is planned to take at least two days. Your sponsor calls you and informs that she will be in the neighborhood tomorrow morning. She asked if it would be possible to inspect and sign off what you have completed so far. What should be your response
questions.option_a: Rush the testing to have the deliverables verified by tomorrow morning
questions.option_b: Accept the meeting request and prepare the required sign-off form
questions.option_c: Tell the sponsor to expect your call as soon as deliverables are verified
questions.option_d: Negotiate with the sponsor to waive quality requirements
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, the team is about to start the Control Quality process to test the deliverables and confirm the deliverables are verified. The verified deliverables are an output from the Control Quality process and an input into the Validate Scope process where they become accepted deliverables after the customer or sponsor accepts them. The project team must first complete the Control Quality process in order for the sponsor to inspect and sign off on the deliverables as part of the Validate Scope process. Therefore, of the choices provided, telling the sponsor to expect your call as soon as deliverables are verified is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: You are leading a project to build a new professional sports stadium, and you are in the process of implementing the stakeholder engagement plan. In order to ensure continued support of the project by the most influential stakeholders, you have arranged for a meet and greet with some of the professional athletes that will be playing in the new stadium. What is the best way for you to determine which stakeholders to invite to the meet and greet
questions.option_b: Meet with the key stakeholders to determine which are the most influential
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process is being carried out. The stakeholder register is one of the inputs to the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process and provides the list of project stakeholders and any information needed to execute the stakeholder engagement plan. The stakeholder register contains stakeholder classification information which will be useful in determining which stakeholders are the most influential and should be invited to the meet and greet. Of the available choices, only the stakeholder register provides stakeholder classifications, making it the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: During project planning, a project manager allocates a dedicated team member to develop and distribute weekly project status reports to project stakeholders. What project document should reflect this repetitive and time-consuming task
questions.explanation: The scenario implies that the project manager is developing the communications management plan which is the primary output of the Plan Communications Management process. Project document updates, such as the project schedule, are among the outputs of that process. According to the scenario, the project manager assigns a dedicated team member to develop and distribute the project status reports since this activity is expected to be recurring and likely time- consuming. The project schedule presents linked activities with planned dates, duration, milestones, and resources. This means the project schedule is the best place to document the repetitive task of the development and distribution of the weekly project status report.

questions.prompt: A 4-week project requires that its members work side-by-side. Two developers, a designer, and a specialist were to work every day for the first 2 weeks. Due to limited computers, only three can work simultaneously. You re-evaluate and see the need for developers for 4 weeks, the designer just for the 1st half and the specialist only for 2nd half of the project. What is the best resource optimization technique to use to re-schedule the activities to accommodate three computers
questions.explanation: Once you re-evaluated the tasks and determined the graphic designer is only needed in weeks 1 and 2, and the e-commerce specialist is only needed in weeks 3 and 4, you discover there is enough slack in the schedule to move the e-commerce specialist to begin work in week 3. This change would accommodate the three-computer constraint because you would have the two developers and the graphic designer on the three computers the first two weeks and the two developers and the e-commerce specialist on the three computers in last two weeks. The technique used to make these changes is called 'resource smoothing.' Resource smoothing is a resource optimization technique that adjusts activities of a schedule to fit predefined resource limits without changing the completion date. In the scenario described, resource smoothing is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A software development project is running behind schedule because the velocity of the project team has been slowed down by a higher than anticipated complexity of the technology used to develop the product. To bring the project back into alignment with its objectives, the project manager implements the planned response by fast tracking some of the testing activities. What should the project manager do next
questions.option_a: Update the schedule using the project management information system (PMIS)
questions.option_b: Submit a change request to update the schedule baseline to reflect the results of fast tracking
questions.option_c: Submit a change request to update the issue log with the final resolution
questions.option_d: Update the risk register to add the newly identified risk of higher than anticipated complexity
questions.explanation: The question implies that the Implement Risk Responses process is underway. Project management information systems (PMIS) are among the tools and techniques of this process. The PMIS can be used to update the project schedule to reflect changes resulting from fast tracking. PMIS can include schedule, resource, and cost software to ensure that agreed-upon risk response plans and their associated activities are integrated into the project alongside other project activities. The incorrect answer choices represent activities which have already been completed or are not likely to be necessary. Therefore, of the available choices, updating the schedule using the project management information system (PMIS) is the next logical step for the project manager to take in this scenario.

questions.prompt: You were hired to lead external implementation projects as well as internal operations projects. Knowing that the distinction between projects and operations is critical, you stress that difference to project staff and stakeholders in your first project meeting. You are asked by a stakeholder if there are any situations that operations and projects will intersect. How should you respond to the stakeholder's question
questions.option_b: Operations and projects may intersect only at the closeout of the project.
questions.option_d: Operations and projects may intersect at each closeout phase.
questions.explanation: The PMBOK® Guide discusses the differences between projects and operations, defining operations as ongoing and a project as temporary. It also discusses the relationship between the two areas and its importance. The guide then lists several examples of when operations and projects may intersect. One example includes closeout phases.

questions.prompt: A project manager is about to close a project that missed its performance goals. The project manager is gathering the information that is most pertinent to the project’s poor performance. Which of the following is least likely to help in this task
questions.explanation: Project team assignments document the team members and their roles and responsibilities for the project. Documentation can include a project team directory and names inserted into the project management plan, such as the project organization charts and schedules. Project team assignments are not typically used as an input into the Close Project or Phase process. Although there may be circumstances where the project team assignments might be helpful, it is the least likely to be used compared to the other choices. The risk report, assumption log, and change log are likely to have valuable information on aspects of the project that were problematic. Since the question is asking for the item that would be 'least' likely to be used for the closing process, project team assignments represent the best response.

questions.prompt: To mitigate recent labor issues and reduce their risk in the future, senior executives are considering alternatives for automating production. One executive is not in favor of using automation at all; a second executive suggests adding some basic automation, while another is in favor of automating the entire production line. Which of the following is the least likely option to be included in the business case
questions.option_b: Do less than the minimum work possible to address the problem
questions.option_c: Do more than the minimum work possible to address the problem
questions.option_d: Do the minimum work possible to address the problem
questions.explanation: A business case should include a set of options to be considered for addressing the business problem or opportunity. Options are alternative courses of action that may be taken by the organization. Doing less than the minimum work possible to address the problem makes the least sense from a business perspective. Taking this option means that the company would expend company resources without receiving the benefits of the project. Why would the company authorize a project that does not produce the intended benefits?

questions.prompt: A project manager has completed the project management plan which has just been reviewed and approved by the project sponsor and others. The project manager now needs to communicate the start of the project along with key milestones, and other relevant information to inform the stakeholders and gain commitment. What is the best way to accomplish these objectives
questions.option_a: Provide copies of the project management plan
questions.option_c: Share the project charter on the company internal website
questions.explanation: The Project Management Professional (PMP)® Examination Content Outline contains a list of tasks for each domain, with which PMP aspirants are expected to be familiar. Task #12 of the planning domain of the Project Management Professional (PMP)® Examination Content Outline states, "Conduct kick-off meeting, communicating the start of the project, key milestones, and other relevant information in order to inform and engage stakeholders and gain commitment." Therefore, of the choices given, conducting a meeting is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A key project deliverable has been outsourced to a vendor. The vendor delivered on contract, and the project manager has completed all activities associated with the Control Procurements process. What should the project manager do next
questions.option_b: Approve the deliverable received from the vendor
questions.explanation: According to the scenario, the Control Procurements process has already been completed. Three of the four answer choices represent activities, which are typically performed during the Control Procurements process and, therefore, can be eliminated. Measuring stakeholder satisfaction is part of the Close Project or Phase process. Therefore, of the choices provided, after completing the activities associated with the Control Procurements process, measuring stakeholder satisfaction is what the project manager should do next.

questions.prompt: A construction company which is very experienced with building apartments has been contracted to build a strip mall which is a relatively new type of project for the organization. The project is now concluded, and the project team has documented a significant amount of new knowledge gained throughout the project. Where would a project manager find this information when planning their future similar project
questions.explanation: The lessons learned register is an output of the Manage Project Knowledge process. It is then used as an input and updated as an output in many processes throughout the project. The knowledge gained throughout the project can then be used to improve performance for the current project. At the end of the project or phase, the information is transferred to an organizational process asset called a lessons learned repository for use in future projects. A future project manager working on a subsequent project can access the knowledge gained on past projects from the lessons learned repository.

questions.prompt: You have been hired midway into project execution to replace the previous project manager. The project is in disarray: discussions on changes frequently become shouting matches between the project team members, no one seems to take responsibility, and the project management plan is incomplete. As a result, the project is far behind schedule. What is your best course of action
questions.option_a: Use management reserves to cover for the lost time
questions.option_b: Seek help from the project management office (PMO)
questions.option_c: Fire the project team members and outsource the work
questions.option_d: Revise the project management plan and have it approved
questions.explanation: The scenario describes a project in execution after the initial project manager leaves. Since the project management plan is incomplete, and discussions on changes to deliverables frequently become shouting matches between the project team members, it is reasonable to assume that the change management plan is the main cause of the flawed or even missing change control process on the project. Reviewing the project management plan, specifically the change management plan, and making necessary modifications to ensure changes are properly managed on the project from now on is essential for the project to proceed.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project and is currently preparing the project planning documents. In support of this process, the project manager must gather information to determine how work will be executed to accomplish the project objectives. What is the best way for the project manager to perform this task
questions.option_c: Schedule a meeting with the appropriate stakeholders
questions.explanation: The question implies the project manager is conducting the Develop Project Management Plan process. One of the tools and techniques associated with the Develop Project Management Plan process is the use of meetings. Meetings may be used to discuss the project approach, determine how work will be executed to accomplish the project objectives and establish the way the project will be monitored and controlled. Of the answer choices provided, gathering the appropriate stakeholders together for a meeting is the best way for the project manager to perform the task at hand.

questions.prompt: You are developing the project management plan for your current project. You want to know if there is any relevant information from past projects that can help you with your task. What should you do
questions.option_c: Meet with a project manager who managed similar past projects
questions.option_d: Check the enterprise environmental factors for lessons learned from past projects
questions.explanation: The question is asking where to find information on lessons learned from past projects. The lessons learned repository is an organizational process asset which serves as a store of historical information about lessons learned from past projects. Therefore, researching the organizational process assets is the best answer to the question asked.

questions.prompt: A project is completing late and over budget. There were no major issues, quality problems, or changes, and only a few of the risks needed response actions. The project manager is trying to determine what has caused the problem. Which of the following is most likely to point to the root cause
questions.explanation: The basis of estimates contains supporting documentation outlining the details used in establishing project estimates such as assumptions, constraints, level of detail, ranges, and confidence levels. The basis of estimates is used as an input to the Close Project or Phase process implied by the scenario to evaluate how the estimation of durations, cost, resources, and cost control compared to the actual results. Sometimes, selecting the correct answer requires the prospective PMP aspirants to use the process of elimination of the worst answer choices. According to the scenario, the project did not encounter any major issues, quality problems, or changes, and only a few risks have realized necessitating the implementation of the response plans. This information may help eliminate the answer choices related to the scope, schedule, cost, quality, risks, etc., leaving the basis of estimates as not ideal but the only plausible answer choice.

questions.prompt: One of your project team members, who was responsible for several key deliverables, has suddenly left the company. You overhear a heated debate among the team members over what to do next. It sounds as if the team is split - half want to hire a new team member; the other half want to contract out the work. As the project manager, you want to resolve this conflict in a way that solves the problem and keeps the team unified. Which of the following approaches should you take
questions.option_a: Tell the team that you are in charge and will take disciplinary action if a consensus is not reached
questions.option_b: Agree with the team that a resource is needed, and urge one side to back down for the sake of agreement
questions.option_c: Ask the team members to find a compromise that would be acceptable to both sides
questions.option_d: Request that the team researches different options, presents ideas, and works out a solution
questions.explanation: PMI prefers problem solving and collaboration when it comes to resolving issues or conflicts, as it is a win-win conflict resolution technique that typically leads to greater buy-in and the most lasting solution. The 'collaborate/problem solve' approach emphasizes open dialogue, cooperation, and incorporating multiple viewpoints to find the best outcome for the project. In this scenario, the project manager sends the project team on a fact-finding mission, which is a good way to help the team discuss the issue more objectively. Getting the team together in a problem-solving session to present their ideas and work out a solution is more likely to satisfy both sides and keep the team unified; therefore, the 'Collaborate/Problem Solve' approach is the 'best' answer choice. All other approaches are temporary solutions resulting in recurring conflicts.

questions.prompt: As the project manager, you are in the process of assisting the project sponsor with creating the project charter for a project which will design and launch a new social media platform. In order to create the project charter, you need to get a better understanding of the project's objectives. What should you do to obtain this information
questions.option_c: Check the project needs analysis documents
questions.option_d: Review the business case and benefits management plan
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Develop Project Charter process is underway. Business documents including the business case and the benefits management plan serve as an input to the Develop Project Charter process. These business documents are sources of information about the project's objectives and how the project will contribute to the business goals. Therefore, to get a better understanding of the project's objectives, you should review the business case and benefits management plan.

questions.prompt: A project manager is in the process of developing the stakeholder register. As the first step in this process, she needs to create a list of potential stakeholders and decides to use a data gathering technique called brain writing. What is the first thing to do when using that technique
questions.option_a: Convene a data gathering meeting with the participants
questions.option_b: Develop your own template for a stakeholder register
questions.option_c: Send a list of questions to participants before the creativity session
questions.option_d: Consult the stakeholder management plan to understand how the stakeholders are to be identified
questions.explanation: Data gathering techniques that can be used for the Identify Stakeholders process include questionnaires and surveys, brainstorming, and brain writing. Brain writing is a refinement of brainstorming that allows individual participants time to consider the questions individually before the group creativity session is held. Therefore, the first step of brain writing is providing the participants with the questions before the creativity session. This process will allow the participants to be better prepared which will make the meeting more effective than a traditional brainstorming session.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project to develop a prototype diesel engine. One problem that was identified and documented was the corrosion of engine parts which were stored in an offsite storage facility. The parts were refinished and coated with a penetrant to inhibit corrosion in the future. The project manager is confident that these measures will solve the problem. With respect to tools and techniques, what else should the project manager do in this situation
questions.option_d: Determine if the problem has been resolved
questions.explanation: The question describes a situation where a problem occurred on the project, and measures were taken to resolve the issue suggesting that problem solving was used in this case. Problem solving may use a set of tools that helps the project manager to solve problems that arise during Control Resources, the process implied by the scenario. Although the question states that the project manager is confident that the steps taken will solve the problem, there is no way to determine the effectiveness of the solution at this point. The solution should be checked to ensure that the problem has been indeed fixed. In this case, that might involve periodically inspecting the parts and reapplying the penetrant as necessary. The final step of the problem solving process is to check the solution which is all that remains to be completed in this scenario with respect to tools and techniques. The incorrect answer choices represent actions that do not involve tools and techniques or have already been completed.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a project which has experienced numerous quality issues. These issues threaten the successful completion of the project objectives. In order to improve the performance of the project, the project manager schedules a retrospective with the project team and relevant stakeholders. Which of the following topics is least likely to be discussed
questions.option_a: What to incorporate into the ongoing project and what to include in future projects
questions.option_b: What are the project objectives and the roles and responsibilities of the stakeholders
questions.option_c: What elements of the project have gone well and what could be improved upon
questions.option_d: What should be added to the organizational process assets (OPAs)
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Control Quality is being performed, and a retrospective, among the tools and techniques used in this process, is being held to determine how to address the quality issues that have been encountered during the project. A retrospective is a scheduled review to record lessons learned which can then be documented in the lessons learned register to improve the future performance of the project. Appropriate topics of discussion for the retrospective would be successful elements of the project, what could be improved, what to incorporate in the ongoing project and what to include in future projects, and what to add to the organizational process assets. The purpose of a project kick-off meeting is to communicate the objectives of the project, gain the commitment of the team for the project, and explain the roles and responsibilities of each stakeholder. Note, the question is asking which answer choice represents a topic that is 'least' likely to be discussed. Therefore, of the available choices, the objectives of the project and the roles and responsibilities of each stakeholder are 'least' likely to be discussed in this scenario.

questions.prompt: A vendor on your project informs you that they will be unable to perform the work as contracted after some equipment required for the project was destroyed in a hurricane. The vendor cites a clause in the contract that is designed to address the inability of a party to execute their responsibilities due to extraordinary factors that were outside the control of the party? What clause does the vendor refer to
questions.explanation: A force majeure clause is a common contractual clause that frees parties, bound by a contract,

questions.prompt: The project team is just starting the process of developing approaches to involve project stakeholders based on their needs, expectations, interests, and potential impact on the project. In support of this process, the project manager is advising the team to review information regarding the project purpose, objectives, and success criteria. What is the best way for the team to obtain this information
questions.option_c: Examine the stakeholder engagement assessment matrix
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the team is starting the Plan Stakeholder Engagement process. The project charter is one of the inputs into this process. The project charter contains information on the project purpose, objectives, and success criteria. Therefore, reviewing the project charter is the most appropriate action for the project team to take in this scenario.

questions.prompt: After a change request to expand the scope of the project has been submitted to the change control board (CCB), the project manager is reassigned to another project. The CCB approves the change and communicates the status of the change request to the newly hired project manager. What should the new project manager do first
questions.option_a: Direct the project team to implement the change
questions.option_b: Wait for further changes and implement them all together
questions.explanation: All proposed changes to a project must be handled according to the organization's change control process via a change request. In this scenario, a change request to expand the scope of the project was submitted to the change control board for review and analysis of its impact on the project. Once approved, the status of the change request is updated in the change log. The next logical step would be implementing the change. However, the scenario describes a new project manager who is likely not familiar with the change control processes defined for this particular project. One should look for the answer choice that best addresses the question asked under the circumstances provided in the scenario. If consulting the change management plan was not among the answer choices, then directing the project team to implement the change would have been the best answer. However, since each organization and each project within the organization may have its own change control process (which is specified in the change management plan), consulting this plan should be the first thing the newly assigned project manager should do. For example, the change management plan may require the project manager to inform particular stakeholders about the approved change so that they may take actions assigned to them as part of the procedure prior to the implementation of the change. Therefore, in this case, consulting the change management plan is the best answer among the choices given in the situation described by the scenario.

questions.prompt: A project manager and team are creating the work breakdown structure (WBS) for a project. The project charter indicates that the project will take approximately seven years to complete. Since some of the deliverables will not be produced until far into the future, the project team has determined that further decomposition is not possible at this point for many of the subcomponents. How should the project team resolve this situation
questions.option_a: Seek the advice of someone that has worked on similar projects in the past
questions.option_b: Perform a product analysis to decompose the work packages into activities
questions.option_c: Divide and then subdivide the project scope and project deliverables into smaller, more manageable parts
questions.option_d: Employ rolling wave planning as a way to move forward with project planning
questions.explanation: Rolling wave planning is an iterative planning technique in which the work to be accomplished in the near term is planned in detail, while the work in the future is planned at a higher level. Rolling wave planning is a form of progressive elaboration applicable to work packages, planning packages, and release planning when using an agile or waterfall approach. Work can exist at various levels of detail depending on where it is in the project life cycle. During early strategic planning when information is less defined, work packages may be decomposed to the known level of detail. As more is known about the upcoming events in the near term, work packages can be decomposed into activities. Of the available choices, rolling wave planning is the best technique to use to address the issue raised by the question.

questions.prompt: The sponsor asks the project manager for the project's resource breakdown structure (RBS) so that she can present it to senior management during an upcoming meeting. All the subsidiary management plans are complete, but the RBS is not yet developed. Which process must be completed in order to produce the RBS for the sponsor
questions.explanation: Estimate Activity Resources is a process of estimating team resources and the type and quantities of materials, equipment, and supplies necessary to perform project work. The resource breakdown structure (RBS) is one of the outputs of this process. The RBS is a hierarchical representation of resources by category and type. One of the approaches to answering questions on the PMP exam is elimination. The incorrect answer choices can be eliminated for the reasons provided in their respective explanations. Of the choices provided, only Estimate Activity Resources is the process that should be completed in order to produce the RBS for the sponsor, thus making Estimate Activity Resources the best answer to the question asked. Note, RBS is mainly referred to as a risk breakdown structure. However, in its Glossary, the PMBOK® Guide mentions RBS as also a resource breakdown structure.

questions.prompt: A project manager sends an email to the product developers notifying them of a critical defect found by the customer and asking them to report back to her so that she could update the sponsor. The next day, the project manager is bombarded by the developers stopping by her office to defend their efforts and point the blame elsewhere. What could the project manager have done differently to obtain a more positive response from the developers
questions.option_d: Asked the developers to update the sponsor directly
questions.explanation: Some resources say as much as 90% of a project manager's time is spent on communication. Effective communication builds a bridge to diverse stakeholders and team members and develops the relationships necessary for successful project outcomes. The matching of media to messages is an important aspect of effective communication and effective management. For example, deciding when it is better to communicate in writing versus orally, or deciding when to prepare an informal memo versus drafting a formal report is an important aspect of managing communications on projects. According to the scenario, the developers did not respond well to the email regarding the defect. By communicating the information verbally, for example setting up a meeting, video call, or conference call, the project manager could have incorporated opportunities for interaction and obtained a more positive response from the developers.

questions.prompt: While reviewing the issue log of the project you are leading, you suspect there is a pattern of errors and defects. You and your project team want to determine the causes and frequency of these errors and defects in the system. In particular, you are interested in identifying and ranking the vital few sources that are responsible for causing the majority of the problems. Which of the following tools will help you the most
questions.explanation: You want to group the error and defects captured in the issue log into categories. These categories would represent the source of the issues. Of the choices provided, the Pareto chart is the only answer choice that allows for the ranking of grouped data points (in this case the error and defects) represented by categories. A Pareto chart is a special form of a vertical bar chart, or a histogram, where each bar represents a source of a problem. The height of the bar reflects the relative frequency of the corresponding source. A histogram, such as the Pareto chart, is an example of the data representation tools and techniques that can be used during the Control Quality process described in the scenario. Hence, of the available choices, the Pareto chart is the best tool to use to identify and rank the vital few sources that are responsible for causing the majority of the problems and is, therefore, the best answer to the question asked. While the topic of this question is only briefly discussed in the PMBOK® Guide, the Project Management Professional (PMP)® Examination Content Outline, June 2015, covers knowledge and skills with which PMP aspirants are expected to be familiar. Various quality control tools and techniques are among these topics.

questions.prompt: A project manager is leading a product development project within a centralized organizational structure. One of the project team members has left the organization and needs to be replaced if the project goals are to be met. The project manager has identified an employee within the organization that has the necessary skill set to fill the vacancy. What should the project manager do first
questions.option_a: Submit a change request to update the schedule baseline
questions.option_b: Negotiate with the employee's functional manager
questions.option_c: Discuss the staffing situation with the project sponsor
questions.option_d: Reassign the identified employee to the project team
questions.explanation: The question indicates that the project exists within a centralized (also called functional) organizational structure. Within a centralized organization, the project manager has little or no direct control over resources. If the project manager needs to acquire an employee from within the company, the project manager will need to negotiate the reassignment of the employee to the project. Therefore, of the available choices, the project manager should first negotiate with the employee's functional manager to release the employee to work on the project.

questions.prompt: You are halfway through a project, which has experienced several setbacks due to unanticipated problems. Based on recent feedback, you are concerned that critical support for the project among key stakeholders is eroding. You want to bring the stakeholders together for a meeting to address any concerns and rebuild support for the project. Which of the following meetings would be the most beneficial for the key stakeholders to attend
questions.explanation: The question suggests that the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process is underway. Various meetings can be beneficial in this process including the iteration retrospective. An iteration retrospective is a specialized type of meeting used on projects with an agile framework and is conducted at the end of each iteration. The purpose of a retrospective is to cover project progress thus far and determine what worked well, what did not work well, and how project performance can be improved going forward. Inviting the key stakeholders to the iteration retrospective will allow the project team to address any concerns the stakeholders may have and develop a plan to ensure the problems encountered do not recur. This approach should be beneficial in reassuring the stakeholders that positive action is being taken to bring the project back into alignment with project goals. Additionally, the iteration retrospective is held throughout project execution at the end of each iteration. The ability of stakeholders to influence the project is typically highest during the initiating stage and gets lower as the project progresses. The incorrect answer choices represent meetings that are held at the end of the project and/or are not relevant to the Manage Stakeholder Engagement Process. Note, even though in "pure" agile environment, the iteration retrospective meetings are typically held by and for the team members with an agile leader (such as a scrum master or an agile coach), other project stakeholders can be invited as necessary. Therefore, while not ideal, of the available choices, the iteration retrospective would be the most beneficial for key stakeholders to attend as it pertains to the Manage Stakeholder Engagement process.

questions.prompt: A software developer on an agile project team has discovered that adding a second computer monitor has increased productivity by reducing the time spent opening and closing screens. The company has extra monitors in storage, so cost is not an issue. As an agile coach, you want this practice to be adopted by the rest of the team members. What is your best course of action
questions.option_a: Discuss the best practice with the project team during the project retrospective
questions.option_b: Have the project team member share the best practice during the upcoming iteration retrospective
questions.option_c: Request the PMO to establish a new corporate policy that all developers should use two monitors
questions.option_d: Pull the extra monitors out of storage and assign one additional monitor to each developer
questions.explanation: A common problem with establishing continuous improvement during project execution is that lessons learned can become an afterthought as the project team focuses on the work of producing the project's deliverables. Best practices and knowledge gained may be recorded in the lessons learned register but that information is of no value if it is not shared with the project team to improve the performance of the project. When an agile approach is used, the Control Schedule process implied by the question is concerned with conducting retrospectives. An iteration retrospective is a scheduled review in which best practices can be shared to improve the performance of future iterations. Having the team member that discovered the best practice share her experience with the project team will bring credibility as she is already having success with improved productivity. Therefore, of the available choices, having the project team member share the best practice during the upcoming iteration retrospective is the best answer to the question asked.
